idea-plugin.name: Completor
idea-plugin.description:
Dynamic Autocompletion from External Data Sources

Tired of manually typing keys, IDs, or constants defined in external XML or JSON files? Completor bridges that gap by bringing those values directly into your IDE's autocomplete suggestions.

Configure it once with your data file and powerful XPath / JSONPath queries, and get instant, context-aware suggestions across your project.

Key Features

  • ✅ Provides autocomplete suggestions from any XML or JSON file.
  • ✅ Powerful configuration via a dedicated settings panel.
  • ✅ Simple Mode: Works out-of-the-box with conventional structures.
  • ✅ Advanced Mode: Supports separate XPath / JSONPath queries for keys and values, allowing it to adapt to almost any file structure.
  • ✅ Optionally insert the "value" instead of the "key" – perfect for localization or templates.
  • ✅ A user-friendly file chooser for easy data source selection.

Usage Examples

Example 1: Simple XML (Key in Attribute, Value in Text)

If your XML file uses a common structure where the key is an attribute and the value is the element's text.


<!-- data.xml -->
<definitions>
    <constant id="const.pi">3.14159</constant>
    <message id="msg.welcome">Welcome!</message>
</definitions>

Configuration:

  • Key Path Query: //constant[@id]|//message[@id]
  • Value Path Query: (Leave blank - Completor will automatically use the element's text)

Example 2: Advanced XML (Dual Queries)

When your keys and values are in completely different parts of the document.


<!-- config.xml -->
<config>
    <keys>
        <key>auth.token.google</key>
        <key>theme.name</key>
    </keys>
    <values>
        <value>google-secret-token-123</value>
        <value>Darcula</value>
    </values>
</config>

Configuration:

  • Key Path Query: //keys/key/text()
  • Value Path Query: //values/value/text()

Example 3: Simple JSON (Conventional Key/Value Pairs)

If your JSON contains an array of key-value objects.


{
  "feature_flags": [
    { "key": "feature.new.dashboard", "value": "Enables the new UI" },
    { "key": "feature.beta.access", "value": "Grants beta access" }
  ]
}

Configuration:

  • Key Path Query: /feature_flags
  • Value Path Query: (Leave blank - Completor will automatically look for "key" and "value" properties)

Example 4: Advanced JSON (Dual Queries)

When your keys and values are in separate arrays or different parts of the JSON.


{
  "routes": {
    "names": ["route.home", "route.profile"],
    "paths": ["/home", "/user/profile"]
  }
}

Configuration:

  • Key Path Query: $.routes.names[*]
  • Value Path Query: $.routes.paths[*]
